examples.title: Algorithm: Circular Queue Operations
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the core operations of a circular queue using modulo arithmetic for wraparound
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CircularQueue
INPUT: capacity (max size of queue)
OUTPUT: Queue operations

DATA STRUCTURE
 Queue[0 to capacity-1] // Array to store elements
 front: Integer // Index of front element
 rear: Integer // Index of rear element

INITIALIZE()
 front ← -1
 rear ← -1
 RETURN queue

ISFULL(queue, capacity)
 // One slot kept empty to distinguish full from empty
 RETURN ((rear + 1) % capacity) = front

ISEMPTY(queue)
 RETURN (front = -1 AND rear = -1)

ENQUEUE(queue, value, capacity)
 IF ISFULL(queue, capacity) THEN
 PRINT "Queue is full"
 RETURN
 END IF

 IF ISEMPTY(queue) THEN
 front ← 0
 rear ← 0
 ELSE
 rear ← (rear + 1) % capacity // Circular increment
 END IF

 queue[rear] ← value
 PRINT value + " enqueued successfully"

DEQUEUE(queue, capacity)
 IF ISEMPTY(queue) THEN
 PRINT "Queue is empty"
 RETURN NULL
 END IF

 value ← queue[front]

 IF front = rear THEN
 // Only one element, reset queue
 front ← -1
 rear ← -1
 ELSE
 front ← (front + 1) % capacity // Circular increment
 END IF

 RETURN value

FRONT(queue)
 IF ISEMPTY(queue) THEN
 PRINT "Queue is empty"
 RETURN NULL
 END IF
 RETURN queue[front]
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the key operations of a circular queue. The critical concept is using modulo arithmetic (index % capacity) to implement wraparound behavior. When rear reaches the end of the array, it wraps back to 0 if there are empty slots. The isFull condition uses ((rear + 1) % capacity == front) to check if the queue is full while keeping one slot empty to distinguish from the empty state.

examples.title: Python: Circular Queue Implementation
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of a circular queue with all operations
examples.code:
class CircularQueue:
 """
 Circular Queue implementation using array.
 Uses modular arithmetic for wraparound behavior.
 """
 
 def __init__(self, capacity):
 """Initialize queue with given capacity."""
 self.capacity = capacity
 self.queue = [None] * capacity
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 
 def is_full(self):
 """Check if queue is full. One slot kept empty."""
 return (self.rear + 1) % self.capacity == self.front
 
 def is_empty(self):
 """Check if queue is empty."""
 return self.front == -1 and self.rear == -1
 
 def enqueue(self, value):
 """Add an element to the rear of the queue."""
 if self.is_full():
 print(f"Queue is full! Cannot enqueue {value}")
 return False
 
 if self.is_empty():
 # First element
 self.front = 0
 self.rear = 0
 else:
 # Circular increment
 self.rear = (self.rear + 1) % self.capacity
 
 self.queue[self.rear] = value
 print(f"Enqueued: {value}")
 return True
 
 def dequeue(self):
 """Remove and return element from front of queue."""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is empty! Cannot dequeue")
 return None
 
 value = self.queue[self.front]
 self.queue[self.front] = None
 
 if self.front == self.rear:
 # Last element - reset queue
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 else:
 # Circular increment
 self.front = (self.front + 1) % self.capacity
 
 print(f"Dequeued: {value}")
 return value
 
 def peek(self):
 """Return front element without removing it."""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is empty")
 return None
 return self.queue[self.front]
 
 def display(self):
 """Display current queue state."""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is empty")
 return
 
 print("Queue elements:", end=" ")
 if self.front <= self.rear:
 # Normal case
 for i in range(self.front, self.rear + 1):
 print(self.queue[i], end=" ")
 else:
 # Wrapped around case
 for i in range(self.front, self.capacity):
 print(self.queue[i], end=" ")
 for i in range(0, self.rear + 1):
 print(self.queue[i], end=" ")
 print()


# Demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
 cq = CircularQueue(5)
 
 print("=== Circular Queue Demo ===\n")
 
 # Enqueue elements
 for val in [10, 20, 30, 40]:
 cq.enqueue(val)
 
 cq.display()
 print(f"Front element: {cq.peek()}")
 print(f"Is full: {cq.is_full()}")
 
 # Dequeue one element
 cq.dequeue()
 
 # Enqueue more - demonstrates wraparound
 cq.enqueue(50)
 cq.enqueue(60)
 
 cq.display()
 print(f"Is full: {cq.is_full()}")
 
 # Fill the queue
 cq.enqueue(70)
 
 print(f"Is full: {cq.is_full()}")
 
 # Dequeue and enqueue to show wraparound
 cq.dequeue()
 cq.enqueue(80)
 
 cq.display()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates all core circular queue operations. The key feature is using modulo arithmetic (self.rear + 1) % self.capacity to achieve wraparound behavior. When the queue wraps around, the rear index goes back to 0, allowing efficient space reuse. The isFull condition keeps one slot empty to distinguish between full and empty states.

examples.title: C: Circular Queue Implementation
examples.description: Complete C implementation of a circular queue using structures and functions
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates a complete circular queue with modular arithmetic for wraparound. The key operations are enqueue (adds at rear with circular increment) and dequeue (removes from front with circular increment). The isFull check uses ((rear + 1) % MAX_SIZE == front) to determine if the queue is full while keeping one slot empty. This implementation efficiently reuses space by allowing both front and rear pointers to wrap around.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for all operations (enqueue, dequeue, isFull, isEmpty)
